symptoms.symptom_name: Discrete painful oval ulcers confined to non-keratinised mucosa (inner lip and cheeks), sparing palate/gingiva/tongue

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Aphthae characteristically occur on movable non-keratinised mucosa; sparing of keratinised sites is typical.
Recurrent intraoral HSV and primary herpetic gingivostomatitis favour keratinised mucosa (hard palate, attached gingiva).
A prodromal burning/tingling sensation preceding ulceration is typical of aphthae.
HSV lesions begin as clustered vesicles that rupture; the absence of a vesicular stage argues against HSV.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recurrent episodes every few months, stress-associated

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A recurrent, self-resolving, stress-linked pattern is diagnostic of recurrent aphthous stomatitis and points to considering haematinic screening.

symptoms.symptom_name: No fever and no cervical lymphadenopathy

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Primary herpetic gingivostomatitis is typically febrile with tender lymphadenopathy; their absence argues against a systemic infective cause.
The absence of systemic viral symptoms and rash argues against an enteroviral cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: Inability to maintain oral fluids for ~18 hours due to odynophagia

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reflects severity and the secondary dehydration risk rather than the diagnosis itself; the driver of management and safety-netting.

symptoms.symptom_name: Intraoral examination: three well-circumscribed oval ulcers up to 5 mm with yellow-grey pseudomembranous base and erythematous halo on non-keratinised mucosa; keratinised mucosa clear; no vesicles

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The morphology and distribution are characteristic of minor aphthae — a clinician examination finding, sealed as it cannot be obtained via chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Serum haematinics (iron studies, B12, folate) — screening in recurrent disease

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal haematinics support idiopathic recurrent aphthous ulceration; a deficiency would identify a treatable contributor. Not required for acute management — a routine outpatient screen for the recurrent pattern.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Serum iron studies, vitamin B12 and folate (routine, outpatient — for the recurrent pattern, not acute management)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Recurrent aphthous ulceration warrants a one-off haematinic screen to identify a correctable deficiency. It does not change acute management and should not be treated as urgent.
The clinical picture is characteristic of aphthous ulceration; routine viral testing is not indicated and would over-investigate a benign presentation.
